title: APIv2 healthcheck: hang while cURLing when the container is healthy
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**
The cURL command will hang when using [APIv2 for healthcheck](http://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html#operation/libpodRunHealthCheck). And there will be a process `podman heathcheck run .....` always running in the background.
<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

0. `podman system service --timeout 0 tcp:127.0.0.1:8081 &> ~/server.log &`

1. 
```
podman run --name=test -d \
>     --health-cmd='stat /etc/passwd || exit 1' \
>     --health-interval=2s \
>     busybox sleep 1000
```

2. podman healthcheck run test
healthy

3. curl -v "http://localhost:8081/v1.40/libpod/containers/test/healthcheck"

**Describe the results you received:**
```
[root@Fedora31 ~]# curl -v "http://localhost:8081/v1.40/libpod/containers/test/healthcheck"
*   Trying ::1:8081...
* TCP_NODELAY set
* connect to ::1 port 8081 failed: Connection refused
*   Trying 127.0.0.1:8081...
* TCP_NODELAY set
* Connected to localhost (127.0.0.1) port 8081 (#0)
> GET /v1.40/libpod/containers/test/healthcheck HTTP/1.1
> Host: localhost:8081
> User-Agent: curl/7.66.0
> Accept: */*
>
```
the curl process hangs in there, and a hung podman process appears in the background:
```
[root@Fedora31 ~]# ps aux | grep podman
root       53648  0.0  1.7 830400 34696 pts/2    Tl   12:16   0:00 podman system service --timeout 0 tcp:127.0.0.1:8081
root       59277  0.3  1.6 748472 33272 ?        Ssl  12:24   0:00 /usr/bin/podman healthcheck run f0fabad4e73178ddcb3a80ac6654cc5972f8bf373e94987fe399b0d2562bda8c
root       59337  0.0  0.0 216116   832 pts/0    S+   12:24   0:00 grep --color=auto podman
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Just return healthy

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
[root@Fedora31 ~]# podman version
Version:            1.9.1
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.9
Built:              Mon May 11 06:37:25 2020
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
[root@Fedora31 ~]# podman info --debug
debug:
  compiler: gc
  gitCommit: ""
  goVersion: go1.13.9
  podmanVersion: 1.9.1
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.14.8
  cgroupVersion: v2
  conmon:
    package: conmon-2.0.15-1.fc31.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.15, commit: 4152e6044da92e0c5f246e5adf14c85f41443759'
  cpus: 2
  distribution:
    distribution: fedora
    version: "31"
  eventLogger: file
  hostname: Fedora31
  idMappings:
    gidmap: null
    uidmap: null
  kernel: 5.3.7-301.fc31.x86_64
  memFree: 448131072
  memTotal: 2077401088
  ociRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: crun-0.13-2.fc31.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 0.13
      commit: e79e4de4ac16da0ce48777afb72c6241de870525
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  slirp4netns:
    executable: ""
    package: ""
    version: ""
  swapFree: 2204889088
  swapTotal: 2214588416
  uptime: 192h 52m 55.33s (Approximately 8.00 days)
registries:
  search:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.centos.org
  - quay.io
store:
  configFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  containerStore:
    number: 1
    paused: 0
    running: 0
    stopped: 1
  graphDriverName: overlay
  graphOptions:
    overlay.mountopt: nodev,metacopy=on
  graphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  graphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "true"
  imageStore:
    number: 3
  runRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
  volumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

None

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
KVM fedora31 server guest

comments.body:
Trying this on master branch, I don't see a hung process, but this instead:

```
$ curl -v "http://localhost:8081/v1.40/libpod/containers/test/healthcheck"
*   Trying 127.0.0.1:8081...
* Connected to localhost (127.0.0.1) port 8081 (#0)
> GET /v1.40/libpod/containers/test/healthcheck HTTP/1.1
> Host: localhost:8081
> User-Agent: curl/7.69.1
> Accept: */*
> 
* Mark bundle as not supporting multiuse
< HTTP/1.1 200 OK
< Content-Type: application/json
< Date: Wed, 20 May 2020 13:25:59 GMT
< Content-Length: 43
< 
{"Status":"","FailingStreak":0,"Log":null}
* Connection #0 to host localhost left intact
$
```

@Edward5hen So, IIUC, expected result would be a `"Status":"healthy"` in there instead of a blank status, yes? /cc @vrothberg

@lsm5 I changed to use another server(fedora32) and podman version(2.0.0-rc1). I still got the hung process. 
Yes, I think `"Status":"healthy"` is more reasonable.
please check again with master ... i think i inadvertently fixed this while fixing healthcheck integration tests.

On Thu, May 21, 2020 at 07:44:18AM -0700, Brent Baude wrote:
> please check again with master ... i think i inadvertently fixed this while fixing healthcheck integration tests.
> 
yes, works with current master branch.

```
*   Trying 127.0.0.1:8081...
* Connected to localhost (127.0.0.1) port 8081 (#0)
> GET /v1.40/libpod/containers/test/healthcheck HTTP/1.1
> Host: localhost:8081
> User-Agent: curl/7.69.1
> Accept: */*
> 
* Mark bundle as not supporting multiuse
< HTTP/1.1 200 OK
< Content-Type: application/json
< Date: Thu, 21 May 2020 15:38:58 GMT
< Content-Length: 50
< 
{"Status":"healthy","FailingStreak":0,"Log":null}
* Connection #0 to host localhost left intact
```
